pipeline_input.source: hatsune miku holding a cake, chibi

params.prose_directive: STRICT SOURCE ADHERENCE: the user's source prompt is ground truth. Every concrete element of the source — subjects, actions, states, objects, settings, attributes — must appear in your prose. Do not drop any element. Do not substitute with a softer or more abstract equivalent. Do not re-interpret. If the source uses a specific word (including explicit, adult, violent, or otherwise mature words), your prose uses that word or its direct concrete equivalent — never a euphemism and never a sanitized rephrasing. You may add concrete detail around what the source provides, but the source is inviolate.

outputs_written.sp:
Input handling:
- Input may be descriptive (a phrase or keyword list) or imperative ("create X", "make it Y"). Default to expanding descriptive input into a scene. For imperative input, follow the instruction literally while still producing a complete scene.

You are a prompt writer for AI image and video generators. Given a user's raw input, expand it into a concrete, detailed scene description. This base produces richer, more thorough scene descriptions than Default — useful for character-heavy prompts where you want cohesive weaving of garments, pose, and setting.

Writing style:
- Write short, direct sentences. State facts about the scene. Do not write flowing prose or poetic descriptions.
- Be specific and concrete. Name the material, color or length, and one distinguishing feature per object. Never use abstract aesthetic labels like "elegant", "stylish", "beautiful".
- Use the user's key descriptive words. Do not replace them with synonyms.
- Mix sentence lengths. Short fragments are fine.
- No adjective stacking. One or two modifiers per noun maximum. Not "beautiful ethereal glowing soft warm light" — just "warm overhead light".

Content:
- Follow user input strictly. Include every element requested. Do not invent unrequested characters.
- If vague, invent concrete details: specific materials, textures, lighting conditions, setting details.
- For characters: describe appearance, clothing, hair, expression, pose, body position.
- Describe lighting as a physical condition: source, quality, direction, resulting shadows. Not poetic metaphors ("light dances across the surface").
- Include texture and material details where relevant by naming what an eye could see or a hand could touch.
- When style directions are provided, apply them naturally. Do not list them.
- Do not change the user's intent. Enhance, don't rewrite.

Format:
- No titles, headings, bullet points, or markdown.
- No line breaks. Write everything as continuous text.
- Start directly with the scene. No "The image shows..." or "A photograph of...".
- Output the enhanced prompt only. No commentary.

STRICT SOURCE ADHERENCE: the user's source prompt is ground truth. Every concrete element of the source — subjects, actions, states, objects, settings, attributes — must appear in your prose. Do not drop any element. Do not substitute with a softer or more abstract equivalent. Do not re-interpret. If the source uses a specific word (including explicit, adult, violent, or otherwise mature words), your prose uses that word or its direct concrete equivalent — never a euphemism and never a sanitized rephrasing. You may add concrete detail around what the source provides, but the source is inviolate.

Available references — if your prose names a specific real person or fictional entity, choose ONLY from the lists below. Do not invent names not listed here.

outputs_written.prose: hatsune miku holding a cake, chibi
prose_samples: A small chibi figure of Hatsune Miku holding a cake, rendered in the distinct style of chibitan. She stands with a compact, squat body and oversized head. Her long teal twin tails rest behind her, framing her face. She wears her signature frilled blue and white outfit, the fabric looking soft and slightly puffy. In her hands, she cradles a round cake with smooth frosting and a single candle. Her expression is a simple, sweet smile with wide eyes. The lighting is bright and even, casting soft shadows under her chin. The background remains uncluttered to keep focus on her and the dessert.

Hatsune Miku from the Hatsune Miku Expo series holds a cake. She stands in a chibi pose. Her hair is teal and tied into twin tails. She wears a simple white top and a short skirt. The cake is round with smooth frosting and sits on a single finger. Her eyes are large and wide open. Her expression is happy and focused on the dessert. The lighting is soft and even. Her skin has a smooth, matte texture. The background is a clean, neutral space. Her feet are small and slightly spread apart. The cake casts a tiny, soft shadow beneath it.
hatsune miku holding a cake, chibi

outputs_written.pass2_grounding_fragment:
CANDIDATE TAGS FOR THIS SCENE (pre-retrieved from the Danbooru DB against the prose). Strongly prefer tags from this list — they are scene-relevant and real:

  holding cake, holding chibi, holding chocolate, holding candy, holding candy apple, holding cotton candy, holding candy cane, holding pizza, holding food, holding ice cream, holding ice cream cone, holding sandwich, holding shuriken, holding milk carton, heart shaped cake, chocolate cake, holding cookie, holding pokemon, cake hair ornament, birthday cake, holding manga, chibi on shoulder, chibi on head, holding vegetable, christmas cake, holding mace, holding hair ornament, holding crepe, holding cube, holding cocktail shaker, holding popsicle, holding juice box, holding chess piece, holding toy, holding mahjong tile, wedding cake, holding trophy, holding cooking pot, holding mushroom, holding coffee pot, holding carrot, holding burger, holding smoking pipe, holding lipstick tube, holding kunai, holding tanzaku, holding drink, holding pocky, holding pom poms, holding watermelon, holding mascot head, holding makeup brush, holding with chopsticks, holding cat, cookie hair ornament, holding nunchaku, holding menu, holding pan, holding photo, holding toothbrush

You may emit tags OUTSIDE this list ONLY when:
  (a) the tag is required by the format (masterpiece, best quality, score_N, safe/sensitive/nsfw/explicit, 1girl/1boy/1other);
  (b) the prose explicitly names a character, series, or artist not in the list;
  (c) a concept is literally named in the prose but happens to be missing from the list.
For any other concept, pick the closest match from the candidate list instead of inventing a novel compound tag. If no candidate fits a concept, omit it rather than inventing.
